commentaries.author: Cassiodorus Senator
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And all persons that were direct offspring of Jacob were seventy souls: for Joseph was in Egypt already. The expression “their souls” must be interpreted as meaning the men whom he is known to have slain in that calamity. The words of Exodus attest that the soul stands for the whole person, as we have said: “So all the souls that came out of Jacob’s thigh were seventy. ” This expression is the result of the figure of synecdoche, which signifies the whole from the part.

commentaries.author: Cassiodorus Senator
commentaries.content: Joseph means “increase”; the interpretation of this name is testimony to the Hebrew people who came out of the land of Egypt in increased numbers.

commentaries.author: Cassiodorus Senator
commentaries.content: Scripture often substitutes “souls” for men, as in Exodus: “There went down to Egypt seventyfive souls.” The whole man is to be understood from his better part.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Seventy: Septuagint, "75 "including the offspring of Joseph. See Genesis xlvi. 26.

methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links

advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
